hero.eyebrow: AI study companion · K-12 to college
hero.lede: CatoBigato is a warm, witty tutor that explains, quizzes, and graphs — across math, science, and the humanities. Snap a problem, ask in plain English, get a real teacher's answer — CatoBigato adapts to how you think.

pillars.desc: Each lives behind a single nav item up top — but here's what's inside.

tutor.title_em: Catobi listens, thinks, and shows the work.
tutor.desc: A chat-first tutor — attach knowledge bases, keep memory across sessions, draft essays in Co-Writer, switch to a TutorBot specialist per subject.

calculator.desc: From arithmetic to symbolic calculus, with custom user-defined functions you can save and reuse.

learning.desc: Write notes, build question sets, run timed exams, and rework what you missed. Catobi checks; teachers can too.

puzzles.desc: Browse a growing bank of curated problems — or design your own and share them. Version-controlled, exportable.

social.desc: Follow classmates, form study groups, share progress to Google Calendar or WeChat. Competition is opt-in and never loud.

visual.desc: Animated SVG diagrams across math, physics, chemistry, biology, literacy, and arts. Render is in your browser — fast and offline-friendly.

tutor.blurb: Talk to Catobi. Explanations, drafts, and memory across sessions.

calculator.blurb: Six modes in one — from arithmetic to symbolic.

learning.blurb: Notes, sets, exams — your study record in one place.

social.blurb: Friends, groups, and gentle, opt-in study competition.

visual.blurb: Every subject as an animated, interactive SVG.

tutorPreview.desc: Chat with Catobi, summon a TutorBot specialist, attach reading material, draft essays in Co-Writer — every mode shares the same memory of you.
tutorPreview.placeholder: Ask Catobi anything — paste a problem, snap a photo, or just chat.
tutorPreview.example3: Critique the thesis of my essay (attached).
tutorPreview.example4: Quiz me on the French Revolution.

calcPreview.title_pre: Type math like you'd speak it.
calcPreview.desc: Catobi writes back in natural language, beautifully typeset math, and live graphs. Interrupt her any time — argue, follow up, or ask 'wait, why?'
calcPreview.list1: Live graphing and a geometry sketchpad.
calcPreview.list2: Symbolic math — not just numerical answers.
calcPreview.list4: Custom user-defined functions you can save, share, version.
calcPreview.explain: Power rule term-by-term: ∫3x² = x³, ∫2x = x². Don't forget the constant of integration

subjectsStrip.title_pre: From grade school to grad school,
subjectsStrip.desc: Catobi speaks a different language for every field — same warmth, same patience.

flow.desc: More than answers. Catobi teaches the way of thinking that produced them.
flow.step1_desc: Photo of a worksheet, a PDF, or a question typed in your own words.
flow.step2_desc: She figures out what's actually being asked, in whichever language you used.
flow.step3_desc: Step-by-step reasoning — proofs shown, diagrams drawn, units checked.
flow.step4_desc: A short follow-up to make sure it sticks. Never punishing, always playful.
flow.step5_desc: Quiet progress notes you'll actually want to read.

quote.text_pre: "I'm not here to take the test for you — I'm here to make you

visual_math.title: Visual Math
visual_math.subtitle: Interactive geometry, animations, and LaTeX rendering.
visual_math.tip_latex: Tip: Type any math expression. Press Ctrl+Enter to render.
visual_math.tip_geogebra: Tip: Use geometry keywords like triangle, circle, angle, bisector.
visual_math.tip_animate: Tip: Describe a process or transformation to animate.
visual_math.animate_placeholder: e.g. show how a circle area formula is derived
